config.seed_text: The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions.

model.notes: Direct continuation of the current repo's GPT-2 lineage.

records.output_text: To make this work it had to be written down at least 30 years earlier than usual—more or less what you would expect given such an early understanding of your community; but because every part was so similar (such "literary" sources like The Old English Alphabet could also have been taken from books by other peoples), each poem contained three parts which both inspired me and convinced my students I knew better how to write them correctly. After some time with one teacher who wrote poems about vampires he learned through repeated practice using his own personal experience telling us we must follow our culture for all these steps before writing

generation.full_text:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Write the next recursive transformation of the text below. Use the latent anchor tokens only as soft guidance, not as a list to copy verbatim.

LATENT ANCHORS: aren, didn, did, He, are, weren, shall, does, We, couldn, cannot, C

TEXT:
The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions. To make this work it had to be written down at least 30 years earlier than usual—more or less what you would expect given such an early understanding of your community; but because every part was so similar (such "literary" sources like The Old English Alphabet could also have been taken from books by other peoples), each poem contained three parts which both inspired me and convinced my students I knew better how to write them correctly. After some time with one teacher who wrote poems about vampires he learned through repeated practice using his own personal experience telling us we must follow our culture for all these steps before writing

After some time with one teacher who wrote poems about vampires he learned through repeated practice using his own personal experience telling us we must follow our culture for all these steps before writing

records.output_text: anything else…. In addition to that lesson alone being hard enough…I am currently working on making more extensive use out there...and even though its first public release will probably take several months over many different projects....please keep checking back!

generation.full_text:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Write the next recursive transformation of the text below. Use the latent anchor tokens only as soft guidance, not as a list to copy verbatim.

LATENT ANCHORS: To, Captain, to, Can, Ph, Suicide, An, Female, Kitty, Under, Artificial, ー

TEXT:
To make this work it had to be written down at least 30 years earlier than usual—more or less what you would expect given such an early understanding of your community; but because every part was so similar (such "literary" sources like The Old English Alphabet could also have been taken from books by other peoples), each poem contained three parts which both inspired me and convinced my students I knew better how to write them correctly. After some time with one teacher who wrote poems about vampires he learned through repeated practice using his own personal experience telling us we must follow our culture for all these steps before writing anything else…. In addition to that lesson alone being hard enough…I am currently working on making more extensive use out there...and even though its first public release will probably take several months over many different projects....please keep checking back!
